textPlain: Congrats for making it but it feels like being pushed to do it since CLAP was brought forward quite successfully [1][1] https://u-he.com/community/clap/

That's very interesting news. Definitely brought on by CLAP as others have mentioned, but it's interesting to see how this evolves. VST is a pretty complicated standard to support whereas CLAP is much simpler, although the former is much more widely used.
There is a lot of good news in open source audio these days. Also see this video presenting the work done and planned for the future version 4 of Audacity: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QYM3TWf_G38
Before VST3 code was under GPL3 license and GPL2 software (like LMMS) couldn't use it.Also the license change could be caused by competition from CLAP which is very openly licensed.
Wow!! What a huge and wonderful change. Hats off, Steinberg.
This simplifies a ton of things.

His name was Tony Cornell, and between the 1950s and his death in 2010, he was one of the most prolific parapsychologists—which is to say, one who investigates psychic phenomena and other paranormal claims—https://www.stbemucode.online/
no, his name is John Cena!
